cases.passage: You are standing on the surface of the Earth, looking up at the sky. You notice that the stars appear to be at the same position every year, but the position of the Sun and the Moon changes. This is because both the Earth and the Moon are orbiting around the center of mass of the Earth-Moon system. The center of mass is the point where the mass of the Earth and the Moon is equally distributed. The force of gravitation between the Earth and the Moon keeps them in this orbit. Now, imagine a spacecraft orbiting around the Earth. What is the force that keeps the spacecraft in its orbit?

questions.q: What is the force that keeps the spacecraft in its orbit?
questions.explanation: The force that keeps the spacecraft in its orbit is the gravitational force due to the Earth's mass.

questions.q: What is the point where the mass of the Earth and the Moon is equally distributed?
questions.explanation: The point where the mass of the Earth and the Moon is equally distributed is the center of mass.

questions.q: What is the force that keeps the Earth and the Moon in their orbit?
questions.explanation: The force that keeps the Earth and the Moon in their orbit is the gravitational force between them.

questions.q: What is the point around which the Earth and the Moon orbit?
questions.explanation: The point around which the Earth and the Moon orbit is the center of mass of the Earth-Moon system.

questions.q: What is the effect of the gravitational force on the Earth and the Moon?
opts: (A) They move away from each other
questions.explanation: The gravitational force between the Earth and the Moon causes them to orbit each other.

cases.passage: A satellite is launched into a circular orbit around the Earth. The satellite has a mass of 100 kg and the radius of the orbit is 20000 km. The gravitational force between the Earth and the satellite is 100 N. What is the mass of the Earth?

questions.q: What is the type of orbit of the satellite?
questions.explanation: The satellite is in a circular orbit around the Earth.

questions.q: What is the force acting on the satellite?
questions.explanation: The force acting on the satellite is the gravitational force due to the Earth's mass.

questions.q: What is the radius of the orbit of the satellite?
questions.explanation: The radius of the orbit of the satellite is given as 20000 km.

questions.q: What is the mass of the satellite?
questions.explanation: The mass of the satellite is given as 100 kg.

questions.q: What is the gravitational force between the Earth and the satellite?
questions.explanation: The gravitational force between the Earth and the satellite is given as 100 N.
